title: Intelligent Three Level Learning Architecture for Autonomous UAV Swarms in Search and Rescue
abstract: We introduce a three-level learning architecture for autonomous UAV swarms that coordinates reflex, skill and reasoning layers. The evaluation studies task allocation, communication loss and adaptive strategy switching in simulated search-and-rescue missions.

title: ToolAnchor: Anchoring Counterfactual Context to Boost Agentic Tool-use Capability
abstract: ToolAnchor introduces counterfactual context anchors to reduce behavioral inertia when language-model agents encounter unfamiliar tools. Experiments compare tool selection and execution across several agent benchmarks.

title: Signatures of Black Hole Spin in Horizon-Scale Polarimetry
abstract: This work analyzes polarization signatures associated with black-hole spin using horizon-scale very-long-baseline interferometry simulations. It reports observable patterns and discusses modeling assumptions.

title: SPINE: Bridging the Cyber-Physical Gap with Agentic AI
abstract: SPINE is a deployment and debugging framework for agentic control of dual-arm robotic platforms. The paper evaluates operator time, task success and recovery behavior under several manipulation tasks.

title: Photometric Variability and Rotation of Beta Pictoris b from JWST NIRCam Coronagraphic Imaging
abstract: We report photometric variability in the directly imaged super-Jupiter Beta Pictoris b using continuous JWST NIRCam coronagraphic monitoring. The analysis examines periodicity, atmospheric dynamics and observational limitations.

title: Distributed Quantum Sensing with Metropolitan Photonic Links
abstract: The study demonstrates a distributed quantum sensing protocol over metropolitan photonic links. It describes the optical architecture, error budget and measured sensitivity across three network nodes.
